title: Smart masks
abstract: A smart mask includes a face covering, an attachment member, one or more power source(s), an air circulation subsystem, an exhaust subsystem, and a controller. The face covering is configured to cover a face area of a wearer. The attachment member is configured to attach the face covering onto the face area of the wearer. The air circulation subsystem is powered by at least one of the one or more power source(s) and configured to filter outside air and draw the filtered outside air into the face area. The exhaust subsystem is placed below the air circulation subsystem and configured to purge exhaled air out of the face area. The controller is configured to control the air circulation subsystem.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A smart mask, comprising: a face covering configured to cover a face area of a wearer; an attachment member configured to attach the face covering onto the face area of the wearer; one or more power source(s); an air circulation subsystem powered by at least one of the one or more power source(s) and configured to filter outside air and draw the filtered outside air into the face area; an exhaust subsystem placed below the air circulation subsystem and configured to purge exhaled air out of the face area; an audio subsystem powered by at least one of the one or more power source(s) and configured to receive a sound signal wirelessly from a user terminal; and a controller configured to control the air circulation subsystem and the audio subsystem. 2. The smart mask of claim 1, wherein the audio subsystem includes at least (1) a pair of earbuds or (2) a pair of speakers. 3. The smart mask of claim 2, wherein each of the pair of earbuds includes a magnetic portion or a metal portion, the attachment member also includes a pair of magnetic portions or metal portions corresponding to the pair of earbuds, such that when the pair of earbuds are not in use, each of the pair of earbuds is configured to be secured to the corresponding magnetic portion or metal portion of the attachment member magnetically. 4. The smart mask of claim 1, wherein the audio subsystem further includes a microphone configured to receive a voice input, and the controller is further configured to process the voice input to identify one or more voice command(s) for controlling the air circulation subsystem or the audio subsystem. 5. The smart mask of claim 1, further comprising: a network interface configured to connect to a user terminal, wherein when the user terminal is connected to the network interface, the network interface is configured to transmit status data to the user terminal, and to grant the user terminal control of the air circulation subsystem. 6. The smart mask of claim 1 , wherein the air circulation subsystem comprises at least: one or more filter receptacle(s), each of which is configured to receive a filter cartridge for filtering the outside air drawn into the face area; one or more fan(s), each of which is powered by at least one of the one or more power source(s) and configured to draw outside air through a corresponding filter cartridge into the face area; and one or more puck(s), each of which is configured to removably cover the corresponding filter cartridge or fan. 7. The smart mask of claim 6, wherein the one or more fan(s) include a left fan placed on a left side of the face area and a right fan placed on a right side of the face area; the one or more filter receptacle(s) includes (1) a left filter receptacle coupled to the left fan and configured to receive a left filter cartridge, and (2) a right filter receptacle coupled to the right fan and configured to receive a right filter cartridge; and the one or more puck(s) includes a left puck configured to cover the left fan and the left filter cartridge, and a right puck configured to cover the right fan and the right filter cartridge. 8. The smart mask of claim 6, wherein each of the one or more filter receptacle(s) comprises a magnetic portion, configured to hold a metal frame of the filter cartridge in place magnetically. 9. The smart mask of claim 6, wherein each of the one or more filter receptacle(s) has a circular-shaped recess configured to receive a circular-shaped filter cartridge. 10. The smart of claim 6, wherein each of the one or more fan(s) is configured to operate at different speeds, and the controller is configured to control a speed of the one or more fan(s). 11. The smart mask of claim 6, further comprising: one or more ultraviolet light sources powered by at least one of the one or more power source(s) and configured to disinfect an area around the filter cartridge, wherein the controller is further configured control the one or more ultraviolet light source(s). 12. The smart mask of claim 11, wherein each of the one or more ultraviolet light source(s) is coupled to a corresponding one of the one or more puck(s) or a corresponding one of the one or more filter receptacle(s). 13. The smart mask of claim 12, wherein each of the one or more ultraviolet light source(s) is configured to emit an ultraviolet light at different intensities, and the controller is further configured to control an intensity of the one or more ultraviolet light source(s). 14. The smart mask of claim 1, further comprising: a temperature sensor configured to detect a body temperature of the wearer. 15. The smart mask of claim 1, further comprising: a heart rate monitor configured to detect a heart rate of the wearer. 16. The smart mask of claim 1, wherein the one or more power source(s) include one or more rechargeable batteries, and the smart mask further comprises a charging port configured to charge the one or more rechargeable batteries. 17. A filter cartridge configured to fit in a face mask having a fan and a receptacle for receiving a filter for filtering inhale air, comprising: an outer cylindrical frame; an inner cylindrical frame; and a plurality of layers of fabric deposited between the outer cylindrical frame and the inner cylindrical frame configured to filter inhaling air, wherein the inner cylindrical frame is configured to receive a portion of the face mask. 18. The filter cartridge of claim 17, wherein the inner cylindrical frame further includes a plurality of splines configured to receive a male spline portion of the face mask. 19. A filter cartridge, configured to fit at a bottom portion of a face mask having an exhaust outlet, comprising: a crescent-shaped frame that fits a chin area of the face mask; and a plurality of layers of fabric deposited inside the crescent-shaped frame configured to filter exhaling air. 20. A smart mask, comprising: a face covering configured to cover the nose and mouth of a wearer; an attachment member attached or attachable to opposing sides of the face covering and being configured to extend around a head of a wearer, the attachment member having first and second openings therethrough that are configured to enable a wearerâs ears to pass therethrough; first and second air circulation subsystems, the first air circulation subsystem being disposed on a right side of the face covering and the second air circulation subsystem being disposed on a left side of the face covering; and 

 one or more controls associated with the face covering and configured to control one or more operations of the smart mask. 21. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein the first and second air circulation subsystems protrude out from an outer surface of the face covering. 22. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein each of the first and second air circulation subsystems comprise a generally circular puck with a face having a curved or dome shape. 23. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein the face covering has a vertical line of symmetry and the first and second air circulation subsystems and the one or more controls are arranged symmetrically across the line of symmetry. 24. The smart mask of claim 20, further comprising first and second earbuds connected to the face covering or the attachment member such that the earbuds can be positioned within a wearerâs ears. 25. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein the attachment member ha a length that is selectively adjustable. 26. The smart mask of claim 20, further comprising first and second light subsystems, the first light subsystem extending or being disposed around a perimeter of the first air circulation subsystem and the second light subsystem extending or being disposed around a perimeter of the second air circulation subsystem. 27. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein in the one or more controls comprise: a plurality of buttons disposed on the right side of the face covering between the attachment member and the first air circulation subsystem; and a plurality of buttons disposed on the left side of the face covering between the attachment member and the second air circulation subsystem. 28. The smart mask of claim 27, wherein each plurality of button is arranged in a vertical column. 29. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein the face covering comprises a fabric material that is configured to cover the wearerâs nose and mouth and extend under the wearerâs chin and over the wearerâs cheeks. 30. The smart mask of claim 29, wherein the face covering further comprises a sub-covering, the sub-covering being formed of a generally rigid material. 31. The smart mask of claim 30, wherein the sub-covering is sized to cover the wearerâs nose and mouth. 32. The smart mask of claim 30, where in the fabric material extends out from under the sub-covering around a substantial portion of the perimeter of the sub-covering. 33. The smart mask of claim 30, wherein the first and second air circulation subsystems are mounted in the sub-covering. 34. The smart mask of claim 30, wherein the one or more controls are mounted on the sub-covering. 35. The smart mask of claim 30, further comprising a charging port mounted to the sub-covering. 36. The smart mask of claim 20, wherein the face covering comprises a seal disposed on an interior surface thereof, the seal being configured to create a seal between the face covering and the wearerâs face. 37. The smart mask of claim 36, wherein a portion of the seal that is configured to extend over a wearerâs nose is visible when the face covering is worn by a wearer.
